file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module providing utility methods for HP ID (HPID) authentication operations including sign-in, sign-up, account verification, and country selection dismissal. Contains static methods for complete authentication flows used during user onboarding and login processes in the Instant Ink application.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for HP ID authentication operations. Contains methods for dismissing country selection popups, performing sign-in with credentials, completing full sign-in flows, creating new user accounts with sign-up, verifying account codes via email, and executing complete sign-up flows. All methods are static and work with Playwright page objects to interact with HPID authentication pages.
classes.how_to_instantiate: HPIDHelper is used via static methods only. No instantiation needed. Call methods directly like HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where country selector popup may appear

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to dismiss the country selector popup that may appear on HPID pages. Creates an HPIDPage instance with the provided page object. Uses a try-except block to click the first country selector button using hpid_page.country_selector_button.first.click(). If the button is not found or any exception occurs during the click, the exception is silently caught and passed, allowing the flow to continue without interruption. This is a defensive method used to handle optional UI elements that may or may not be present.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for HPID sign-in page

functions.what_it_does: Performs the core HPID sign-in interaction by filling credentials and submitting the login form. Creates an HPIDPage instance. First fills the username field with the provided email using hpid_page.username.fill(email). Then clicks the username form submit button using hpid_page.user_name_form_submit.click() to proceed to the password entry step. Next fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Finally clicks the sign-in button using hpid_page.sign_in.click() to complete the authentication. This method handles the two-step HPID login flow where username and password are entered on separate screens.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for HPID authentication flow

functions.what_it_does: Executes the complete end-to-end HPID sign-in flow including all precondition steps. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to handle any country selection popup that may appear. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the sign-in option button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.sign_in_option(timeout=20000), then clicks it to navigate to the sign-in form. Finally calls HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password) to perform the actual credential entry and form submission. This is the primary method to use for complete sign-in flows in test scenarios.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for HPID sign-up page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address for the new user account

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password for the new user account

functions.what_it_does: Fills out and submits the HPID account creation form to create a new user account. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Fills the first name field with the hardcoded value 'InstantInk' using hpid_page.firstName.fill('InstantInk'). Fills the last name field with the hardcoded value 'User' using hpid_page.lastName.fill('User'). Fills the email field with the provided email address using hpid_page.email.fill(email). Fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Clicks the market checkbox using hpid_page.market.click() to accept terms. Finally clicks the create account button using hpid_page.create_account_button.click() to submit the registration form.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for account verification page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address where verification code was sent

functions.what_it_does: Retrieves the email verification code and submits it to confirm the new HPID account. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the submit code button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.submit_code(timeout=20000). Sets up a retry mechanism with 3 attempts and 10-second wait intervals. In each retry loop, waits for the specified time using page.wait_for_timeout(wait_time * 1000), then calls common.fetch_verification_code(email) to retrieve the verification code from the email system. If a verification code is successfully retrieved, fills the code input field using hpid_page.code.fill(verification_code), clicks the submit button using hpid_page.submit_code.click(), logs a success message with the email and environment details using framework_logger.info(), and breaks out of the retry loop. If an exception occurs during code retrieval, logs a warning with the attempt number and continues to the next retry. If all retries fail and no verification code was obtained, logs an error message and returns early. After successful code submission, waits for the page to fully load using page.wait_for_load_state('load').

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance for HPID sign-up flow

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address for the new tenant user account

functions.what_it_does: Executes the complete end-to-end HPID account creation flow including all steps from initial page load to account verification. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to handle any country selection popup. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the create account button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.create_account_button_data(timeout=20000), then clicks it to navigate to the sign-up form. Calls HPIDHelper.sign_up(page, tenant_email, common.DEFAULT_PASSWORD) to fill out and submit the registration form using the provided email and the default password from common module. Finally calls HPIDHelper.confirm_account_code(page, tenant_email) to retrieve the verification code from email and submit it to complete account activation. This is the primary method for complete user onboarding flows in test scenarios.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper module follows a clean static method pattern where all methods are class-level utilities that do not require instantiation. The module handles the complete HPID authentication lifecycle including sign-in, sign-up, and account verification. Notable patterns include defensive programming with try-except blocks for optional UI elements (dismiss_select_country), retry logic with configurable attempts for email verification code retrieval (confirm_account_code), and composite methods that orchestrate multiple steps (complete_sign_in, complete_sign_up). The module integrates with external dependencies including HPIDPage for UI interactions, common module for verification code fetching and default password, and GlobalState/framework_logger for environment configuration and logging. All methods accept a Playwright page object as the first parameter and perform synchronous operations.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive helper module for landing page interactions, validations, and testing. Provides methods for opening and accessing the landing page, verifying refer-a-friend banners and footnotes, validating page sections and navigation, testing OOBE and MOBE flows, validating hyperlinks in footer, managing cookie settings, validating FAQ links and content, selecting countries, and validating emergency banners with different color schemes. Used extensively for landing page UI testing and validation scenarios.

classes.class_purpose: Provides comprehensive static utility methods for landing page testing and validation. Contains methods for page navigation and access, refer-a-friend banner and footnote verification, section visibility validation, OOBE (Out-of-Box Experience) and MOBE (Mobile Out-of-Box Experience) flow testing including navigation tabs and sections, footer hyperlink validation across multiple external pages, cookie settings validation, FAQ section and link validation with dynamic content handling, country selection, and emergency banner validation with multiple color scheme configurations for different countries. All methods are static and designed for use in automated test scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: LandingPageHelper is used via static methods only. No instantiation needed. Call methods directly like LandingPageHelper.access(page)

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the Instant Ink landing page URL. Uses page.goto() to navigate to the URL stored in common._instantink_url with a 120-second timeout. After navigation completes, waits for the page load state to reach 'load' using page.wait_for_load_state('load') to ensure the page is fully loaded before proceeding.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to access landing page

functions.what_it_does: Opens the landing page and handles initial page setup including privacy banner acceptance and optional banner dismissal. First calls LandingPageHelper.open(page) to navigate to the landing page. Creates a PrivacyBannerPage instance and calls privacy_banner_page.accept_privacy_banner() to accept the privacy consent banner if present. Creates a LandingPage instance. Uses a try-except block to attempt clicking the close banner button using landing_page.close_banner.click(). If the close banner is not found or cannot be clicked, catches the exception and prints an error message but continues execution. This method ensures the landing page is in a clean state for testing.

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Expected number of free months to verify in the RaF banner text. Defaults to 1.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend (RaF) banner is visible on the landing page and contains the expected text about free months. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the RaF banner element using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_banner). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the banner is visible with a 30-second timeout. Retrieves the banner text content using raf_banner.text_content(). Constructs the expected text string as 'You have {expected_months} free month of HP Instant Ink'. Asserts that the expected text is present in the actual banner text using a Python assert statement with a descriptive error message. If the assertion passes, logs a success message with the expected text using framework_logger.info(). Returns True upon successful verification.

Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend (RaF) footnote element is visible on the landing page. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the RaF footnote element using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_footnote). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the footnote is visible with a 30-second timeout. This is a simple visibility check without text content validation.

parameters.name: section_selector
parameters.description: CSS selector string for the section element to check

functions.what_it_does: Verifies whether a specified page section is currently visible within the browser viewport. Uses page.evaluate() to execute JavaScript code in the browser context. The JavaScript function takes the section_selector as a parameter, uses document.querySelector() to find the element, returns false if the element is not found, otherwise gets the element's bounding rectangle using getBoundingClientRect(), and checks if the element is within the viewport by verifying that rect.top is greater than or equal to 0 and rect.bottom is less than or equal to the window inner height or document client height. Returns the boolean result indicating whether the section is in the viewport.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots or performing actions at specific steps. Called with step name, page object, and screenshot_only flag.

functions.what_it_does: Tests the complete Out-of-Box Experience (OOBE) flow on the consumer landing page for desktop/tablet viewports. Wraps all operations in a try-except block for error handling. Navigates to the hardcoded stage1 landing page URL. Attempts to accept the privacy banner with a 10-second timeout, logging success or debug message if not found. Waits for the sign-up header button to be visible with a 200-second timeout, then clicks it. Attempts to accept privacy banner again on the new page. Waits for the HPID page to be visible with a 50-second timeout. Calls the optional callback function with 'signup_function' step name if provided. Logs success message for sign-up function. Returns to the landing page URL and accepts privacy banner again. Tests the How It Works section by clicking the tab, waiting 5 seconds for scroll animation, verifying section visibility, checking if section is in viewport using verify_section_is_in_viewport(), logging results, and calling callback if provided. Repeats similar testing pattern for Instant Paper Plan section, Plans section, and FAQs section - each time clicking the respective tab, waiting for scroll, verifying visibility, checking viewport position, logging results, and calling callback. If any exception occurs during the flow, logs an error message with the exception details.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots or performing actions at specific steps

functions.what_it_does: Tests the complete Mobile Out-of-Box Experience (MOBE) flow on the consumer landing page for mobile viewports. Similar to consumer_landing_page_oobe but uses mobile-specific UI elements. Wraps all operations in a try-except block. Navigates to the stage1 landing page and accepts privacy banner. Waits for and clicks the mobile menu button (hamburger menu), then waits for and clicks the mobile sign-up button. Accepts privacy banner on new page and verifies HPID page visibility. Calls callback for signup function. Returns to landing page and opens mobile menu again. Tests How It Works section by clicking the mobile tab, waiting for scroll, verifying section visibility and viewport position. Opens mobile menu again before each subsequent section test. Tests Instant Paper Plan section using mobile-specific locators and section element. Opens menu and tests Plans section. Opens menu and tests FAQs section using mobile-specific locators. Each section test includes clicking the mobile menu button, clicking the section tab, waiting for scroll animation, verifying visibility, checking viewport position, logging results, and calling callback. Logs error if any exception occurs.

Validates the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section is visible on the landing page for OOBE (desktop/tablet) view. Wraps operations in try-except block. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the never_worry_section element to be visible with a 50-second timeout. Scrolls the section into view if needed using scroll_into_view_if_needed() with 50-second timeout. Logs success message. Calls the optional callback function with 'never_worry' step name if provided. Logs error if exception occurs.

Validates the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section is visible on the landing page for MOBE (mobile) view. Similar to validate_never_worry_section_oobe but uses mobile-specific locator. Wraps operations in try-except block. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the mobe_never_work_section element to be visible with a 50-second timeout. Scrolls the section into view if needed. Logs success message. Calls optional callback if provided. Logs error if exception occurs.

Validates all footer hyperlinks on the landing page by clicking each link, verifying the destination URL, taking optional screenshots, and closing the new tabs. Wraps all operations in try-except block. Creates a LandingPage instance. For HP.com link: waits for visibility, scrolls into view, uses context.expect_page() to capture the new page when link is clicked, waits for new page to load, verifies URL matches hp.com home page using expect().to_have_url() with regex, logs success, calls callback if provided, and closes new page. Repeats similar pattern for Privacy Statement link (verifies hp.com privacy URL with 5-second wait before screenshot), HP Connected Terms of Use link (verifies hpsmart.com TOU URL), Cookie Settings link (verifies hp.com cookies URL with 5-second wait), Your Privacy Choices link (verifies hp.com privacy choices URL), and Instant Ink Terms of Service link (verifies instantink stage1 terms URL). Each link test includes waiting for element visibility, using expect_page context manager to capture new tab, waiting for load state, verifying URL with regex pattern, logging success, calling callback, and closing the new page. Logs error if any exception occurs during validation.

Validates all footer hyperlinks on the landing page for mobile viewport. Identical to hyperlinks_validation but sets mobile viewport size on each new page. Wraps operations in try-except block. Creates LandingPage instance. For each footer link (HP.com, Privacy Statement, Terms of Use, Cookie Settings, Privacy Choices, Instant Ink Terms), waits for visibility, scrolls into view, captures new page with expect_page(), sets viewport size to 400x740 using new_page.set_viewport_size(), waits for load state, verifies URL with regex, logs success, calls callback, and closes new page. The viewport size setting ensures screenshots and validations are performed in mobile view. Logs error if exception occurs.

Validates the cookie settings functionality on the landing page for OOBE (desktop/tablet) view. Wraps operations in try-except block. Creates LandingPage instance. Waits for footer cookies link to be visible, captures new page when clicked using expect_popup(), waits for new page to load, verifies URL matches hp.com cookies page. Scrolls the cookie preference button into view on the new page, waits for it to be visible, and clicks it. Logs success message. Waits 5 seconds for the cookie preference modal to appear. Retrieves the text content of the cookie preference title element. Checks if the title text equals 'Privacy Preference Center' and logs success or error accordingly. Waits for the cookie preference close button to be visible. Calls callback with 'cookie_preferences' step name if provided. Clicks the close button to dismiss the modal. Logs success message. Closes the new page. Logs error if exception occurs.

Validates the cookie settings functionality on the landing page for MOBE (mobile) view. Identical to cookies_settings_oobe but sets mobile viewport size. Wraps operations in try-except block. Creates LandingPage instance. Waits for footer cookies link, captures new page when clicked, sets viewport to 400x740, waits for load, verifies URL. Scrolls cookie preference button into view, waits for visibility, clicks it, logs success. Waits 5 seconds for modal. Retrieves and validates title text equals 'Privacy Preference Center'. Waits for close button visibility. Calls callback if provided. Clicks close button, logs success, closes new page. Logs error if exception occurs. Note: The code has a syntax error at the end with an orphaned expect statement that appears to be leftover code.

Validates all FAQ section links on the landing page by iterating through FAQ sections, expanding questions, and clicking all links within answers to verify they navigate correctly. Creates LandingPage instance and stores original URL. Scrolls FAQ section into view and verifies visibility. Defines a list of FAQ sections with names and IDs: General, Monthly plans, Yearly plans, and Pay As You Print plans. For each section, logs validation start, navigates back to original URL if needed and waits for networkidle, clicks the section tab using data-testid and aria-controls attributes, waits 500ms. Checks if 'Show more' button exists and clicks it if present. Locates all question buttons within the section using aria-controls attribute. Iterates through each question by index, re-locating questions each time to avoid stale elements, clicking the question to expand it, waiting 300ms, getting the aria-controls ID, locating the answer element by ID, verifying answer visibility with 3-second timeout. Within each answer, locates all anchor links, iterates through links by index, re-locating links each time, getting the href attribute, skipping mailto and tel links. For each valid link, clicks it, waits 2 seconds, gets all pages in context, brings last page to front, waits for networkidle with 35-second timeout. Uses nested try-except to check if HP branding image is visible (logs 'HP text found') or if URL contains '/terms' (logs 'Terms of service page found'), then closes the page. If link validation fails, logs warning and continues. After processing all links in an answer, attempts to collapse the question by clicking it again. Uses extensive error handling with try-except blocks at multiple levels to handle dynamic content, stale elements, and navigation issues. If any section fails completely, logs warning and continues to next section. After all sections processed, navigates back to original URL if needed.

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to select. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a country from the country selector on the landing page. Creates LandingPage instance. Clicks the flag icon to open the country selector dropdown using landing_page.flag_icon.click(). Loads locale data by calling load_locale_data() which returns a locale map dictionary. Retrieves the languages list and country code for the specified country from the locale map. Converts the country code to lowercase. Selects the first language from the languages list. Constructs a locale code string in the format 'language-countrycode'. Locates and clicks the country selector element using the data-testid attribute with the constructed locale code using page.locator(f"[data-testid='{locale_code}-selector']").click().

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to validate emergency banner for. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Validates the emergency banner on the landing page has the correct background color and icon for the specified country. Creates LandingPage instance. Locates the emergency banner's parent element using landing_page.emergency_banner.locator('..'). Calls common.get_font_details(banner_element) to retrieve CSS properties including background color. Extracts the detected background color from the returned data. Logs the detected color. Defines a country_configs dictionary mapping country names to their expected banner configurations including name, expected RGB color, and expected SVG path (or None for non-SVG icons). Countries include Finland (Red Minus Banner with rgb(190, 19, 19)), Bulgaria (Orange Alert Banner with rgb(208, 103, 2) and no SVG path), Portugal (Green Done Banner with rgb(28, 122, 23) and checkmark SVG path), Australia (Blue Done Banner with rgb(2, 122, 174) and checkmark SVG path), Norway (White Done Banner with rgb(255, 255, 255) and checkmark SVG path), Poland (Grey Done Banner with rgb(64, 64, 64) and checkmark SVG path), and Estonia (Black Done Banner with rgb(33, 33, 33) and checkmark SVG path). Retrieves the config for the target country. Logs the banner name being tested. Asserts the detected color matches the expected color with descriptive error message. Logs color validation success. If the config has an expected_icon_path (SVG-based banners), locates the SVG path element within the emergency banner, gets the 'd' attribute value, and asserts it matches the expected SVG path with error message. Logs SVG path validation success. If no expected_icon_path (Bulgaria orange banner), locates the element with class 'orangeBannerIcon', asserts the count is greater than 0, and logs success. Logs overall validation success message.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is an extensive helper module with 14 static methods covering comprehensive landing page testing scenarios. The module demonstrates advanced Playwright patterns including new page/tab handling with context.expect_page(), viewport manipulation for responsive testing, JavaScript evaluation for viewport checks, dynamic element handling with retry logic, and extensive error handling with try-except blocks. Notable methods include consumer_landing_page_oobe and consumer_landing_page_mobe which test complete user flows with multiple section interactions, validate_faq_links which handles complex dynamic content with nested loops and stale element recovery, and validate_new_emergency_banner which validates visual styling across multiple country configurations. The module makes heavy use of optional callback parameters for screenshot capture at specific test steps. All methods are static and designed for use in test scenarios without requiring class instantiation. The module integrates with multiple page objects and uses common utilities for data extraction and locale handling.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module providing utility methods for OSS (Online Services Simulator) emulator setup, configuration, and interaction. Contains methods for accessing the OSS emulator, signing in, configuring printer details, selecting app types, starting flows, handling OOBE and post-OOBE flows, accepting agreements, declining HP Plus, handling dynamic security notices, and verifying trial-related UI elements. Used for printer enrollment and onboarding test scenarios.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for OSS emulator operations. Contains methods for navigating to the OSS emulator URL, signing in with HPID, clicking 3-month trial buttons, filling printer configuration details including serial number, SKU, UUID, cloud ID, fingerprint, language and country settings, selecting app types, starting emulator flows, setting up complete OOBE and post-OOBE flows, accepting connected printing services agreements, declining HP Plus offers, continuing through dynamic security notices and value propositions, selecting printer replacement options, activating HP Plus, selecting countries, verifying reminder checkboxes and trial text, and accessing learn more buttons. All methods are static and work with Playwright page objects and printer data objects.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OssEmulatorHelper is used via static methods only. No instantiation needed. Call methods directly like OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page)

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the OSS emulator URL. Uses page.goto() to navigate to the URL stored in common._oss_url. Waits for the page load state to reach 'load' with a 60-second timeout using page.wait_for_load_state('load', timeout=60000) to ensure the page is fully loaded before proceeding.

Signs into the OSS emulator by clicking the dev tab and HPID login button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the dev_tab element using oss_emulator_page.dev_tab.click() to navigate to the developer section. Then clicks the oss_hpid_login button using oss_emulator_page.oss_hpid_login.click() to initiate HPID authentication.

Clicks the 3-month trial button in the OSS emulator. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits 5 seconds using time.sleep(5) to allow the page to stabilize. Clicks the yeti_3m button with a 60-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.yeti_3m.click(timeout=60000) to select the 3-month trial option.

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object containing entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard, and fingerprint attributes

functions.what_it_does: Fills the OSS emulator form with printer configuration details. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Fills the serial_number field with printer.entity_id using oss_emulator_page.serial_number.fill(). Fills the sku field with printer.model_number. Fills the uuid field with printer.device_uuid. Decodes the base64-encoded printer.postcard using base64.b64decode() and decodes to UTF-8 string, then fills the cloud_id field. Decodes the base64-encoded printer.fingerprint, decodes to UTF-8 string, and fills the first fingerprint field using oss_emulator_page.fingerprint.first.fill(). Selects 'completed' option for language_config dropdown using oss_emulator_page.language_config.select_option('completed'). Selects 'completed' option for country_config dropdown.

parameters.name: app_type
parameters.description: Application type to select. If None, uses common._simulator_platform or defaults to 'Gotham'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects the application type in the OSS emulator. Sets app_type to common._simulator_platform if app_type parameter is None, otherwise defaults to 'Gotham' if both are None. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app_tab to navigate to the app configuration section using oss_emulator_page.app_tab.click(). Selects the specified app_type from the app_type dropdown using oss_emulator_page.app_type.select_option(app_type).

Starts the OSS emulator flow by clicking the start button. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the start_flow_button using oss_emulator_page.start_flow_button.click() to initiate the emulator flow.

Executes the complete OSS emulator setup flow for standard OOBE. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate to the emulator. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to sign in with HPID. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select 3-month trial. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill printer configuration details. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select the app type. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the emulator flow. This is a composite method that orchestrates all setup steps in sequence.

Configures the post-OOBE flow settings in the OSS emulator. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app_post_oobe_agreements_features element to navigate to post-OOBE settings using oss_emulator_page.app_post_oobe_agreements_features.click(). Clicks the subscription_checkbox to enable subscription features using oss_emulator_page.subscription_checkbox.click(). Clicks the oobe_checkbox to enable OOBE features using oss_emulator_page.oobe_checkbox.click().

Executes the complete OSS emulator setup flow for post-OOBE scenarios. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate to the emulator. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to sign in. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select 3-month trial. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill printer details. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select app type. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.setup_poobe_flow(page) to configure post-OOBE settings. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the flow. This is a composite method for complete post-OOBE setup.

Accepts the connected printing services agreement. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits for the page to reach 'load' state with a 120-second timeout using page.wait_for_load_state('load', timeout=120000). Clicks the accept_all_button using oss_emulator_page.accept_all_button.click() to accept all connected printing services terms.

Declines the HP Plus offer during the emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the last occurrence of the do_not_activate_hp_plus button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.do_not_activate_hp_plus.last.click(timeout=120000). Then clicks the decline_hp_plus button using oss_emulator_page.decline_hp_plus.click() to confirm the decline action.

Continues past the dynamic security notice screen. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the ds_continue_button with a 200-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.ds_continue_button.click(timeout=200000) to proceed past the dynamic security notice.

Continues past the value proposition screen. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the next_button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.next_button.click(timeout=120000) to proceed past the value proposition page.

Selects the replace printer option and continues. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the second occurrence (index 1) of the replace_printer_radio_button using oss_emulator_page.replace_printer_radio_button.nth(1).click(). Then clicks the continue_button using oss_emulator_page.continue_button.click() to proceed with the printer replacement flow.

Activates HP Plus during the emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the Hp_Plus_Continue_button using oss_emulator_page.Hp_Plus_Continue_button.click() to proceed to the HP Plus activation screen. Then clicks the Activate_HP_Plus button using oss_emulator_page.Activate_HP_Plus.click() to confirm HP Plus activation.

Selects a country in the OSS emulator based on GlobalState configuration. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Locates the country selector element using the country code from GlobalState.country_code with an ID selector page.locator(f"[id='{GlobalState.country_code}']"). Wraps the selection logic in a try-except block. Scrolls the country element into view if needed using select_country.scroll_into_view_if_needed(). Checks if the country element is visible with a 100-second timeout. If visible, logs an info message with the country code using framework_logger.info(), clicks the country element, and clicks the continue_selection button using oss_emulator_page.continue_selection.click(). Waits 10 seconds using time.sleep(10) after selection. If any exception occurs, silently passes to continue execution.

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Expected number of trial months to verify in the text

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the reminder checkbox is visible, checked, and the trial text contains the expected number of months. Locates the reminder checkbox using data-testid 'instant-ink-reminder'. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the checkbox is visible with a 500-second timeout. Verifies the checkbox is checked with a 2-second timeout. Locates the reminder text element using class selector starting with 'styles__CheckBoxText'. Uses expect().to_have_text() with a regex pattern to verify the text matches 'Remind me 2 weeks before my {expected_months}-month trial of HP Instant Ink expires.' with non-breaking spaces. Locates the trial info disclaimer using data-testid 'general-monthly-fee-disclaimer'. Verifies the disclaimer is visible. Verifies the disclaimer text equals 'After the trial, a monthly fee will be automatically charged unless canceled.'

Clicks the learn more button in the OSS emulator. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the learn_more_button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.learn_more_button.click(timeout=120000) to access additional information.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper module provides comprehensive OSS emulator interaction methods for printer enrollment testing. The module follows a static method pattern with 16 methods covering the complete emulator setup and interaction lifecycle. Notable patterns include composite setup methods (setup_oss_emulator, setup_oss_emulator_poobe_flow) that orchestrate multiple steps, base64 decoding for printer credentials (fill_oss_emulator), defensive programming with try-except blocks (country_select), and integration with GlobalState for environment configuration. The module handles both standard OOBE and post-OOBE flows with separate setup methods. Methods use various timeout values (60s, 120s, 200s, 500s) appropriate for different operations. The verify_reminder_checkbox_and_trial_text method demonstrates complex verification with multiple assertions and regex pattern matching. All methods are static and designed for use in test scenarios without requiring class instantiation.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module providing utility methods for overview page operations and validations. Contains methods for verifying cartridge installation messages, checking printer grouping by status, verifying plan suggestion banners, validating plan upgrade information, verifying unsubscribed status cards, clicking shop ink buttons, enrolling printers again, and downloading past invoices. Used for overview page testing and validation scenarios.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for overview page testing and validation. Contains methods for verifying cartridge installation messages for single or multiple cartridge types, checking the number of printers in status-grouped sections, verifying plan suggestion banners for downgrading or upgrading, validating plan upgrade information, verifying unsubscribed status card elements, clicking shop ink buttons and verifying navigation, clicking enroll printer again buttons and verifying enrollment flow, and downloading past invoices with PDF verification. All methods are static and designed for use in automated test scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OverviewHelper is used via static methods only. No instantiation needed. Call methods directly like OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, 'k,c')

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: Comma-separated string of cartridge types to verify (e.g., 'k,c,m,y' or 'cmy')

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that specified cartridge types are visible on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Splits the cartridge parameter by comma to get a list of cartridge types. Defines a cartridge_locators dictionary mapping cartridge type codes ('k', 'c', 'm', 'y', 'cmy') to their corresponding selector strings obtained from overview_page.get_selector(). Iterates through each cartridge type in the list. Strips whitespace from the cartridge type. Checks if the cartridge type exists in the cartridge_locators dictionary, raising a RuntimeError with descriptive message if not found. For each valid cartridge type, waits for the selector to appear with a 20-second timeout using page.wait_for_selector(), then asserts the element is visible with a descriptive error message.

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: First cartridge type or combination to verify

parameters.name: cartridge2
parameters.description: Second cartridge type or combination to verify as fallback

functions.what_it_does: Verifies cartridge installation with fallback to alternative cartridge type. Wraps the verification in a try-except block. First attempts to verify the first cartridge type by calling OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge). If a RuntimeError is raised (indicating the first cartridge type was not found), catches the exception and attempts to verify the second cartridge type by calling OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge2). This method is useful when a printer may have different cartridge configurations and either configuration is acceptable.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Group name or status text to locate the section (e.g., 'Active', 'Inactive')

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Expected number of printers: 'only one' or 'more than one'

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the number of printers in a specific status-grouped section on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Gets all printer status section elements using overview_page.printers_grouped_by_status.all(). Initializes printers_grouped variable to None. Iterates through each section element, checking if the group parameter text is present in the element's inner text. If found, assigns the element to printers_grouped and breaks the loop. Asserts that printers_grouped is not None and is visible, with error message if section not found. Asserts that the group text is present in the section's inner text. Locates all printer selector option elements within the section using overview_page.printer_selector_option and gets them as a list. If number parameter is 'only one', asserts the list length equals 1. If number parameter is 'more than one', asserts the list length is greater than 1. If number parameter is neither, raises an Exception with 'Invalid number of printers' message.

parameters.name: banner_type
parameters.description: Type of banner to verify: 'downgrading' or 'upgrading'. Defaults to 'downgrading'.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a plan suggestion banner is visible and contains appropriate text for the specified banner type. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Gets the plan suggestion banner selector using overview_page.get_selector('plan_suggestion_banner'). Locates the banner element using page.locator(). Waits for the banner to be visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify banner visibility. Retrieves the banner text content and converts it to lowercase. If banner_type is 'downgrading', defines a list of downgrade keywords ('downgrading', 'lower plan', 'reduce', 'save money', 'smaller plan') and asserts that at least one keyword is present in the banner text with descriptive error message. If banner_type is 'upgrading', defines a list of upgrade keywords ('upgrading', 'higher plan', 'more pages', 'increase', 'larger plan') and asserts at least one is present. If banner_type is neither, raises a ValueError. Prints a success message with checkmark and banner type. Returns the banner locator object.

parameters.name: plan
parameters.description: Expected plan page count as string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information on the overview page shows the expected upgraded plan page count. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the plan_information element is visible with a 120-second timeout. Retrieves the inner text of the plan_information element. Removes periods and commas from the text using replace() method. Uses regex re.findall(r'\d+', plan_info_text) to extract all numeric values from the text. Retrieves the second number from the list (index 1) which represents the plan pages. Asserts that the extracted plan pages value equals the expected plan parameter value.

Verifies that all expected elements are visible on the unsubscribed status card. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses Playwright's expect() to verify three elements are visible: unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button with 150-second timeout, unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button with 30-second timeout, and unsubscribed_download_past_invoices with 30-second timeout. This method confirms the status card displays all required action buttons for unsubscribed users.

Clicks the HP Shop Ink button on the unsubscribed status card and verifies it navigates to the correct shop page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Verifies the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses page.context.expect_page() context manager to capture the new tab that opens when the button is clicked. Clicks the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button. Retrieves the new tab page object from the context manager. Uses expect() to verify the new tab URL contains '/shop/pdp' using regex with a 30-second timeout. Closes the new tab using new_tab.close().

Clicks the enroll printer again button and verifies the enrollment flow is initiated correctly. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Clicks the unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new enrollment page that opens. Retrieves the enrollment page object and brings it to front using enrollment_page.bring_to_front(). Wraps the automatic printer updates acceptance in a try-except block. Attempts to call EnrollmentHelper.accept_automatic_printer_updates(enrollment_page) and logs success if it executes. If an exception occurs, logs that the automatic printer updates page was not found or already passed. Creates a ConfirmationPage instance with the enrollment page. Verifies the v3_content_area element is visible on the confirmation page with a 120-second timeout. Closes the enrollment page using enrollment_page.close().

parameters.name: invoices_directory
parameters.description: Directory path where invoice PDF will be saved. Defaults to '/tmp/downloads'.

functions.what_it_does: Downloads past invoices from the unsubscribed status card and verifies the PDF file is created. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Verifies the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices element is visible with a 30-second timeout. Creates the invoices directory if it does not exist using os.makedirs() with exist_ok=True. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new page that opens when the download link is clicked. Clicks the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices element. Retrieves the PDF page object and brings it to front. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3) for the PDF to load. Constructs the PDF file path by joining the invoices_directory with 'invoice.pdf' filename. Saves the PDF using pdf_page.pdf(path=pdf_file). Closes the PDF page. Waits 2 seconds. Asserts that the PDF file exists at the specified path with descriptive error message. Takes a screenshot of the main page and saves it to the invoices directory with filename 'invoice_pdf_file_screenshot.png'. Logs success message with the PDF file path using framework_logger.info().

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper module provides 8 static methods for overview page testing with focus on cartridge verification, printer status grouping, plan suggestions, and unsubscribed user actions. The module demonstrates advanced Playwright patterns including new page/tab handling with context.expect_page(), PDF generation and file system verification, regex text extraction, and defensive programming with try-except blocks. Notable methods include verify_cartridge_installed_message which uses a dictionary mapping for flexible cartridge type verification, number_of_printers_in_section which validates dynamic printer grouping, verify_plan_suggestion_banner which validates banner content with keyword matching, and download_past_invoices_on_status_card which handles PDF download and file verification. The module integrates with multiple page objects (OverviewPage, ConfirmationPage) and other helpers (EnrollmentHelper). All methods are static and designed for use in test scenarios without requiring class instantiation.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive helper module for print history page operations, validations, and billing data verification. Provides methods for selecting billing cycle periods, verifying billing table data, validating currency fields, verifying billing section details, validating all billing cycles data, validating FAQ sections and links, validating no-pens print history page state, downloading invoices, verifying notifications, and validating plan information. Used extensively for print history and billing validation test scenarios.

classes.class_purpose: Provides comprehensive static utility methods for print history page testing and billing data validation. Contains methods for selecting billing cycle periods from dropdowns, verifying billing table data including total pages printed, normalizing and validating currency values across different formats and locales, verifying complete billing section details with all fields, validating multiple billing cycles data in batch, validating FAQ sections with link navigation testing, validating no-pens print history page state, downloading all invoices with retry logic, verifying notification text in print history tables, and validating plan information and current totals. All methods are static and designed for use in automated test scenarios with extensive error handling and logging.
classes.how_to_instantiate: PrintHistoryHelper is used via static methods only. No instantiation needed. Call methods directly like PrintHistoryHelper.verify_billing_table_data(page, 150)

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that selecting a previous billing cycle changes the period title to not contain 'Current'. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Clicks the select_1_option to select the first previous billing cycle. Uses Playwright's expect() with not_to_contain_text() to verify the billing_cycle_period_title does not contain the text 'Current', confirming a previous period was selected.

parameters.name: period_index
parameters.description: Billing cycle period index to select: 1, 2, or 3

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific billing cycle period from the dropdown by index. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Logs 'Opened billing cycle dropdown'. Uses conditional logic to click the appropriate option based on period_index: if 1 clicks select_1_option, if 2 clicks select_2_option, if 3 clicks select_3_option, otherwise raises ValueError with message indicating valid values are 1, 2, or 3. Logs the selected period index. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table_button is visible with a 10-second timeout, confirming the table has updated. Logs 'Billing table updated after period selection'.

parameters.name: total_pages_printed
parameters.description: Expected total pages printed value to verify

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the total pages printed value in the billing table matches the expected value. Logs 'Starting billing table data validation'. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Retrieves the inner text of the total_pages_printed element. Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract numeric values from the text. Checks if the numbers list is empty, raising ValueError with descriptive message if no numbers found. Asserts that the first number in the list equals the expected total_pages_printed parameter, with descriptive error message showing expected vs actual values and the full text.

functions.docstring: Helper method to get text from a page element
functions.what_it_does: Retrieves text content from a page element given either a locator object or selector string. Checks if the selector has an 'inner_text' attribute using hasattr(). If it does (meaning it's already a locator object), calls selector.inner_text() directly and returns the result. If it doesn't (meaning it's a string selector), uses page.locator(selector).first to get the first matching element, calls inner_text() on it, and returns the result. This method provides flexible text extraction that works with both locator objects and selector strings.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Currency value to normalize (can be string, number, or None)

functions.what_it_does: Normalizes currency values to a consistent format for comparison. If value is falsy (None, empty string, etc.), returns '0.00'. Converts value to string, strips whitespace, and performs multiple replace operations to remove currency symbols and normalize decimal separators: replaces comma with period, removes dollar sign, removes euro symbol, removes pound symbol, removes non-breaking space (\xa0), and removes regular spaces. Checks if the normalized value is in a list of empty/null indicators ('-', '', '—', 'N/A', 'n/a') and returns '0.00' if so. Wraps the float conversion in a try-except block: attempts to convert the normalized string to float, formats it to 2 decimal places as a string using f-string, and returns the result. If conversion fails, returns the normalized string as-is. This method handles various currency formats, locales, and edge cases.

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Selector string or locator object for the currency field

parameters.name: expected
parameters.description: Expected currency value (can be int in cents or string)

parameters.name: partial_match
parameters.description: If True, checks if expected value is contained in actual text. If False, requires exact match. Defaults to False.

functions.what_it_does: Validates that a currency field contains the expected value with flexible matching. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.get_text_from_selector(page, selector) to retrieve the actual text from the element. Checks if expected is an integer, and if so divides it by 100 to convert from cents to dollars. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.normalize_currency_value(expected) to normalize the expected value. If partial_match is True, replaces commas with periods in the actual text and asserts that the expected normalized value is contained in the actual text, with descriptive error message. If partial_match is False, calls normalize_currency_value() on the actual text and asserts exact equality with the expected normalized value, with descriptive error message showing expected, actual normalized, and raw actual values. This method handles various currency formats and provides flexible exact or partial matching.

parameters.name: expected_values
parameters.description: Dictionary containing expected billing values with keys like total_pages_printed, pages, rollover, additional, plan_price, overage_price, previous, charges, tax, total

functions.what_it_does: Verifies all billing section fields match expected values from a dictionary. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. For each field in expected_values dictionary, performs specific validation: If 'total_pages_printed' key exists, extracts numbers from total_pages_printed element text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(), gets the first number, and asserts it equals the expected value. If 'pages' key exists, extracts numbers from plan_pages_text element, gets first number, and asserts equality. If 'rollover' key exists and value is not '-', extracts numbers from rollover_pages_text, gets first number, and asserts equality. If 'additional' key exists and value is not '-', extracts numbers from additional_pages_text, gets first number, and asserts equality. If 'plan_price' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with plan_price_text selector, expected value, and partial_match=True. If 'overage_price' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with overage_description_text selector, expected value, and partial_match=True. If 'previous' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with previous_billing_text selector and expected value. If 'charges' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with current_billing_text selector and expected value. If 'tax' key exists and value is not '-', wraps in try-except to call validate_currency_field() with tax-amount selector, logging warning if element not found. If 'total' key exists, calls validate_currency_field() with current_total_text selector and expected value. All assertions include descriptive error messages with expected and actual values.

parameters.name: expected_data
parameters.description: List of dictionaries, each containing billing_cycle key and expected values for that cycle

functions.what_it_does: Verifies billing data for multiple billing cycles by iterating through expected data and validating each cycle. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Iterates through each row dictionary in the expected_data list. For each row, converts the 'billing_cycle' value to integer and subtracts 1 to get 0-based index. Logs validation message with the billing cycle number. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Logs 'Opened billing cycle dropdown'. Locates all billing cycle option elements using print_history_page.elements.billing_cycle_options. Clicks the nth option based on the billing_cycle_index using billing_options.nth(billing_cycle_index).click(). Logs the selected option number. Waits 1 second using page.wait_for_timeout(1000) for the table to update. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.verify_correct_billing_section(page, row) to validate all fields for the current billing cycle. This method enables batch validation of multiple billing cycles in a single call.

Validates FAQ section functionality on the print history page including question expansion, answer visibility, and link navigation. Creates instances of PrintHistoryPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage, and DashboardSideMenuPage. Clicks faq_question_1 to expand the first question. Verifies faq_answer_1 is visible with 30-second timeout. Uses page.expect_popup() to capture new window when faq_terms_of_service_link is clicked. Verifies the popup URL contains '/terms' with 30-second timeout. Closes the popup. Clicks faq_question_2 to expand the second question. Verifies faq_answer_2 is visible. Clicks faq_overview_link and verifies navigation to overview page by checking status_card_title visibility. Navigates back to print history using side_menu.print_history_menu_link.click(). Clicks faq_question_2 again to expand it. Clicks the first update_plan_link and verifies navigation to update plan page by checking page_title visibility. Navigates back to print history. Clicks faq_question_3 and verifies faq_answer_3 visibility. Clicks faq_question_4, verifies faq_answer_4 visibility, clicks the last update_plan_link, verifies update plan page navigation, and returns to print history. This method comprehensively tests FAQ interaction and navigation flows.

Validates the print history page state when no pens (print jobs) exist. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Verifies that print_history_section is visible with 30-second timeout. Verifies that page_title is visible with 30-second timeout. Then verifies that several elements are NOT visible with 30-second timeout: print_history_card_title, how_is_calculated_link, total_printed_pages, plan_pages_bar, and plan_details_card. This confirms the page shows the basic structure but hides detailed print history elements when no print data exists.

Clicks the download all invoices button with retry logic and verifies navigation to invoices page. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Implements a retry loop with 60 attempts. In each attempt, wraps operations in try-except block. Clicks print_history_table_button to expand the table. Uses expect() to verify download_all_invoices_button is visible with 5-second timeout. If successful, breaks out of the loop. If exception occurs and it's the 59th attempt (last retry), raises exception with message 'Download All Invoices button is not visible after maximum retries'. Otherwise logs info message with retry count, waits 60 seconds using time.sleep(60), reloads the page using page.reload(), and verifies print_history_card_title is visible with 90-second timeout before next retry. After successful button visibility, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab when download_all_invoices_button is clicked. Retrieves the new tab object. Asserts that 'invoices' is present in the new tab URL with descriptive error message. This method handles cases where invoice generation may be delayed.

parameters.name: notifications_text
parameters.description: Expected notification text to find in the print history table

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific notification text appears in the print history table. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Checks if the print_history_table is hidden using is_hidden(). If hidden, clicks print_history_table_button to expand the table. Verifies the print_history_table is visible with 30-second timeout. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3) for the table content to fully load. Retrieves the inner text of the print_history_table. Asserts that the notifications_text parameter is present in the table content with descriptive error message showing expected text and actual table content.

functions.method_signature:
@staticmethod
def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: Expected plan price value as string

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: Expected plan pages value as string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the plan information displays the correct price and page count. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Verifies the print_history_card is visible with 90-second timeout. Retrieves the text content of the plan_price_text element. Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract numeric values from the text. Asserts that the first number (index 0) equals the expected plan_value with descriptive error message. Asserts that the second number (index 1) equals the expected plan_pages with descriptive error message. This method validates both the plan price and page allowance are displayed correctly.

parameters.name: total_value
parameters.description: Expected current total value as string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the current total amount displayed on the print history page. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Verifies the print_history_card is visible with 90-second timeout. Retrieves the text content of the first current_total_text element using .first.text_content(). Calls common.extract_numbers_from_text() to extract numeric values from the text. Asserts that the first number (index 0) equals the expected total_value with descriptive error message showing expected and actual values.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the most comprehensive helper module in the batch with 13 static methods covering extensive print history and billing validation scenarios. The module demonstrates advanced patterns including currency normalization across multiple formats and locales (normalize_currency_value), flexible text extraction from locators or selectors (get_text_from_selector), complex billing data validation with dictionary-driven expected values (verify_correct_billing_section), batch validation of multiple billing cycles (verify_all_billing_cycles_data), retry logic with page reload for delayed invoice generation (click_download_all_invoices), and comprehensive FAQ testing with navigation validation (validate_faq_on_print_and_payment_history). The module integrates with multiple page objects (PrintHistoryPage, DashboardSideMenuPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage) and common utilities for number extraction. Notable utility methods include normalize_currency_value which handles various currency symbols and formats, and validate_currency_field which provides both exact and partial matching with automatic cents-to-dollars conversion. All methods include extensive error handling, descriptive assertions, and logging for debugging. The module is designed for complex billing and payment history validation scenarios in automated tests.

key_reusable_methods: HPIDHelper.complete_sign_in — Complete HPID sign-in flow with credentials
HPIDHelper.complete_sign_up — Complete HPID account creation flow with email verification
HPIDHelper.confirm_account_code — Retrieve and submit email verification code
LandingPageHelper.access — Open landing page and handle privacy banner
LandingPageHelper.consumer_landing_page_oobe — Test complete OOBE flow with all sections
LandingPageHelper.consumer_landing_page_mobe — Test complete MOBE flow with mobile UI
LandingPageHelper.hyperlinks_validation — Validate all footer hyperlinks
LandingPageHelper.validate_faq_links — Validate FAQ section links with dynamic content
LandingPageHelper.validate_new_emergency_banner — Validate emergency banner colors by country
OssEmulatorHelper.setup_oss_emulator — Complete OSS emulator setup for OOBE
OssEmulatorHelper.setup_oss_emulator_poobe_flow — Complete OSS emulator setup for post-OOBE
OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator — Fill printer configuration with base64 decoding
OssEmulatorHelper.verify_reminder_checkbox_and_trial_text — Verify trial reminder UI elements
OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message — Verify cartridge visibility
OverviewHelper.verify_plan_suggestion_banner — Verify plan suggestion banner with keyword matching
OverviewHelper.click_enroll_printer_again_and_verify_enrollment — Re-enroll printer flow
OverviewHelper.download_past_invoices_on_status_card — Download and verify invoice PDF
PrintHistoryHelper.verify_all_billing_cycles_data — Batch validate multiple billing cycles
PrintHistoryHelper.verify_correct_billing_section — Validate complete billing section details
PrintHistoryHelper.normalize_currency_value — Normalize currency across formats and locales
PrintHistoryHelper.validate_currency_field — Validate currency with exact or partial match
PrintHistoryHelper.validate_faq_on_print_and_payment_history — Comprehensive FAQ validation
PrintHistoryHelper.click_download_all_invoices — Download invoices with retry logic
